data.title: Super Micro jumps as internal review finds no misconduct; to replace CFO
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) was up around 13% on Monday after an independent special committee found no evidence of misconduct by the board of directors or audit cozsxmmittee.

No restatement of reported financials is expected based on the findings on the review. Super Micro’s balance sheet came under the scanner after its auditor resigned citing concerns over the company's governance, transparency, and internal control measures.

The artificial intelligence server maker has since appointed BDO USA as its new independent auditor and submitted a plan with the Nasdaq to achieve compliance and avoid getting delisted.

The independent review found no evidence to support the statements made Ernst & Young in its resignation letter as Super Micro’s (SMCI) auditor.

The investigation took over three months and did find some lapses in processes. As part of the investigating committee’s recommendations, Super Micro will appoint a new chief financial officer, chief compliance officer and general counsel, and has named Kenneth Cheung, previously the vice president of finance, as its new chief accounting officer.

Super Micro (SMCI) expects to become current with its financial reports within the discretionary period as part of its compliance plan to Nasdaq. It does not expect any restatements of its quarterly reports for the fiscal year 2024 or for prior fiscal years.

data.title: Construction spending rises 0.4% M/M in October
data.description:
Construction spending edged up 0.4% in October to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of $2,174.0B, higher than the consensus estimate of 0.2% and a sharply higher than the 0.1% rise in September, the U.S. Census Bureau reported on Friday.

The October figure is 5.0 percent above the October 2023 estimate of $2,071.1 billion.

During the first ten months of this year, construction spending amounted to $1,814.8 billion, 7.2 percent above the $1,693.2 billion for the same period in 2023.

Spending on private construction was at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of $1,676.4 billion, 0.7 percent above the revised September estimate of $1,664.7 billion.

Residential construction was at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of $934.0 billion in October, 1.5 percent above the revised September estimate of $920.3 billion.

Public construction spending was $497.6 billion, 0.5 percent below the revised September estimate of $500.0 billion, the U.S. Census Bureau said.

data.title: SA Charts: November 2024 EV Trends—BYD leads, XPeng rebounds, Li Auto & NIO stay resilient
data.description:
The electric vehicle (EV) market is experiencing rapid growth, with manufacturers ramping up production to meet increasing demand.

BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) continues to dominate with unmatched delivery volumes and robust Y/Y growth, while Xpeng (NYSE:XPEV) stands out with the highest M/M growth among peers, signaling a strong recovery. Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI) and NIO (NYSE:NIO) experienced minor setbacks in M/M performance but maintained positive Y/Y momentum, reflecting resilience in a competitive EV market.

Here's the latest monthly delivery data from BYD, Li Auto, Xpeng, and NIO, highlighting key trends, market dynamics, and growth opportunities in this fast-evolving sector.

Li Auto (LI)

  • Deliveries: 48,740 units.
  • Month-over-Month (M/M) Growth: -5.3%, following October's decline of -4.2%, indicating continued softness in monthly performance.
  • Year-over-Year (Y/Y) Growth: +18.8%, maintaining a positive yearly trend despite the monthly slowdown.

XPeng (XPEV)

  • Deliveries: 30,895 units.
  • Month-over-Month (M/M) Growth: +29.2%, the highest among peers, reflecting a strong rebound.
  • Year-over-Year (Y/Y) Growth: +54.2%, highlighting significant yearly growth and market share gains.

NIO (NIO)

  • Deliveries: 20,575 units.
  • Month-over-Month (M/M) Growth: -1.9%, indicating minor seasonal weakness.
  • Year-over-Year (Y/Y) Growth: +28.9%, maintaining steady yearly growth in a competitive EV market.

BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF)

  • Deliveries: 506,804 units.
  • Month-over-Month (M/M) Growth: +0.8%, showing stability after October's notable 19.8% growth.
  • Year-over-Year (Y/Y) Growth: +67.9%, showcasing unmatched yearly growth in the EV sector.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: UPST, STLA, MNSO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures climbed on Monday, setting a positive tone for December after a standout month for the markets.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Despite missing the consensus mark in FQ3, MINISO Group (NYSE:MNSO) shares soared 16%, driven by robust financial performance and operational growth. Revenue increased by 23% Y/Y to RMB 12.28B, supported by a 19% rise in average store count and modest same-store sales growth. The company achieved a record gross margin of 44.9%, up from 41.8% in the same period last year, while adjusted EBITDA grew 12.4% and adjusted net profit rose 6.9%. Guofu Ye, Founder, Chairman, and CEO, expressed confidence, stating, "We are well on track to reach our annual target." As of September 30, 2024, MINISO operated 7,420 stores globally, adding 859 net new locations in the first nine months of the year.
  • Cloudflare (NET) surged 7% after Morgan Stanley upgraded its rating to Overweight from Equal-Weight, citing a path to 'sustainable' 25% to 30% revenue growth over the next few years. This growth is expected to come from multiple product cycles, the increasing role of artificial intelligence at the edge, and a fully ramped-up enterprise sales force. 'We believe the stock will sustain a premium valuation on higher estimates as growth remains strong for longer,' the analysts noted.
  • Just one day before its FQ3 earnings, Okta (OKTA) shares rose over 4% after being upgraded to Overweight from Equal-Weight by Morgan Stanley, with the price target raised to $97 from $92. The firm's analysts noted that they see the demand environment 'stabilizing,' with competition headwinds easing and new products gaining traction. 'Our latest reseller checks support this view, pointing to improving demand and net share gain vs. competitors,' the analysts wrote. They also highlighted positive channel conversations about Okta Identity Governance (OIG), with expectations that the offering could reach $100M in annual contract value (ACV) by Q4.
  • Gap (GAP) shares climbed nearly 5% after CEO Richard Dickson’s leadership was credited with shifting the company’s strategy from simply “fixing fundamentals” to one of “continuous improvement,” earning an upgrade from J.P. Morgan to Overweight from Neutral. J.P. Morgan analyst Matthew Boss highlighted that Dickson’s strategy focuses on increasing efficiency through better inventory management, marketing, and operational savings, as well as reinvesting to fuel long-term growth. Management is targeting low-to-mid-single-digit sales growth, driven by the expansion of the total addressable market for Gap, accelerated growth at Old Navy, and post-reset improvements at Athleta. Following the upgrade, Boss raised his price target for Gap by 7% to $30 and increased his FY25 and FY26 EPS estimates, as well as his operating margin projection to 7.9% by 2026.

Biggest stock losers

  • Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) shares tumbled 8% after CEO Carlos Tavares announced his resignation, marking a sudden exit for the prominent automotive leader. The company, which owns brands like Peugeot, Jeep, and Fiat, cited differences between Tavares and the board as the reason for his early departure, initially expected in 2026. An interim committee led by Chairman John Elkann will oversee operations until a new CEO is appointed in the first half of 2025. Tavares’ departure comes amid mounting challenges, including a September profit forecast cut and anticipated cash burn of up to €10 billion, driven by sluggish U.S. sales and high inventory levels in the North American market.
  • Toast (NYSE:TOST) shares fell over 3% after Goldman Sachs downgraded the stock to neutral from buy, citing high valuations and limited clarity following its recent rally. Analyst William Nance highlighted that, with the stock surging over 135% this year, the risk-reward profile now appears more balanced. He noted that near-term fundamentals are expected to remain relatively stable. The price target was set to $45, suggesting a potential upside of 3.4% from the last closing price.
  • Upstart Holdings (UPST) lost more than 10% after J.P. Morgan analyst Reginald Smith downgraded the stock to Underweight from Neutral, citing concerns over valuation and growth in fintech names. Smith noted that Upstart’s shares appear to be “priced to perfection” despite expectations for improved lending volumes and gain on sale margins in 2025, driven by lower benchmark rates and a better third-party funding outlook. However, he prefers to wait until after the Q4 earnings season for a more attractive entry point.

data.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene discloses buys of AMD, Alphabet, Microsoft, others
data.description:
• A new congressional trading form filed by Congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene disclosed that she recently bought positions in a slew of several companies, including AMD (NASDAQ:AMD), Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT).
  • According to the form, the 50-year-old Congresswoman representing the 14th district in Georgia purchased stakes in technology companies such as AMD, Alphabet, ASML (ASML), Microsoft, Lam Research (LRCX) and Nvidia (NVDA) on November 25. The value of the stakes were between $1,001 and $15,000 each, according to the disclosure form.
  • Greene also purchased stakes in Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B), Charles Schwab (SCHW), Costco (COST), Duke Energy (DUK), GSK (GSK), Home Depot (HD), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), J.P. Morgan (JPM), Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY), NextEra Energy (NEE) and UPS (UPS). The value of these stakes were also between $1,001 and $15,000 each.
  • In August, Congresswoman Greene disclosed purchases of several tech stocks, including Nvidia.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Airbus's plane deliveries are said to have jumped past 80 in November
data.description:
• Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) in November delivered more than 80 commercial planes to customers, the highest November tally since 2018, Reuters reported on Monday, citing industry sources.
  • The deliveries show progress toward the planemaker’s year-end goal of about 770 planes, but many analysts don’t expect Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) to hit that target.
  • Its year-to-date total is about 640 deliveries. Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) in December last year delivered 112 planes.
  • The company for the past few years has grappled with shortages of parts and labor as demand for air travel recovered after the pandemic.
  • The company will report its official November number on December 6.

data.title: Sleep Number holder Stadium Capital nominates four for board
data.description:
Sleep Number's (SNBR) largest shareholder Stadium Capital nominated four directors amid a proxy fight with the mattress retailer.

Stadium Capital, which is Sleep Number's (SNBR) largest holder with an 11.7% stake, nominated Patrick A. Hopf, Jeffrey T. Jackson, Jessica M. Prager and Kevin Baker to the company's board, according to a letter sent to SBNR's board on Monday.

The letter comes after Stadium a week ago threatened a new proxy fight with the mattress retailer. Stadium said it wanted an executive chairman appointed to run the company as CEO Shelly Ibach exits the company, which Sleep Number (SNBR) announced late last month.

"We did not come to this decision lightly, as we strongly prefer private engagement with the management teams and boards of our portfolio companies on ideas for value creation and improved governance," Stadium Capital wrote in the letter.

Sleep Number (SNBR) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha email request for comment.

Stadium last September threatened a proxy fight and then came to an agreement with Sleep Number (SNBR) to add two directors to the board.

Shares of Sleep Number (SNBR) fell 0.8% on Monday.

data.title: Airline stocks look to take off with holiday travel: See 10 of SA’s top quant picks
data.description:
As the holiday season approaches, many investors are forecasting an uptick in consumer travel, with individuals heading to visit family and friends or taking year-end vacations—trends that signal a possible busy seasonal travel period.

As a result, commercial airline stocks are drawing increased attention as the potential holiday travel surge gets underway. In response, Seeking Alpha has compiled a list of the top 10 U.S. airline stocks based on its quantitative ratings for investors. See the list below:

No. 10: Southwest Airlines Co (LUV), quant rating of 3.29.

No. 9: Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT), quant rating of 3.36.

No. 8: Sun Country Airlines Holdings (SNCY), quant rating of 4.43.

No. 7: Joby Aviation (JOBY), quant rating of 4.48.

No. 6: Delta Air Lines (DAL), quant rating of 4.64.

No. 5: American Airlines Group (AAL), quant rating of 4.72.

No. 4: Frontier Group Holdings (ULCC), quant rating of 4.81.

No. 3: Alaska Group Holdings (ALK), quant rating of 4.82.

No. 2: SkyWest (SKYW), quant rating of 4.94.

No. 1: United Airlines Holdings (UAL), quant rating of 4.97.

Year-to-date price action: LUV +12.1%, ALGT -0.9%, SNCY -8.5%, JOBY +34.6%, DAL +58.6%, AAL +5.7%, ULCC +6.8%, ALK +34.6%, SKYW +119.8%, and UAL +134.7%.

Moreover, for a more diversified approach towards the commercial airline space investors can further analyze the U.S. Global Jets ETF (NYSEARCA:JETS) which is a fund that provides exposure to a group of 57 aviation holdings.
